initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> They offered 50 ZUP, which is a good profit. Since my goal is to sell for at least 40 ZUP, and 50 is significantly above that, I will accept this trade. I have made two proposals already, so I will accept the other player's proposal.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Deal! I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for my X. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag seems valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP when my cost was 40 ZUP is optimal and directly advances my payoff.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered 50 ZUP, and I accepted, so it was likely to be accepted.
4. Consistency: OK. My reason for accepting is consistent with accepting the trade and the message reflects the acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only made two proposals and am now accepting, adhering to the rule limits.
</critique>
